summary: This article proposes a role-typed reference contract for multimodal generation agents, addressing the limitations of flat URL lists. The proposed system classifies reference roles, describes visible traits in prompts, and attaches media to provider inputs. The review finds the concept sound and well-articulated, with good potential for improving multimodal agent reliability, though direct empirical validation is limited to internal testing.
strengths: Addresses a critical and practical problem in multimodal AI generation.
Proposes a well-defined and extensible reference taxonomy.
Provides clear examples of prompt binding and provider implications.
Highlights common failure modes and offers solutions.
weaknesses: Lacks independent empirical validation; relies on internal GenFlick audits.
The novelty is primarily in the systematic structuring of an existing problem rather than a fundamentally new technical approach.
The 'validation' section describes internal testing but does not provide details on the scale or rigor of this testing.
suggestions: Include a small, reproducible example or pseudocode demonstrating the agent workflow and provider adaptation.
Quantify the improvement in reliability or user experience observed in the internal audit, if possible.
Discuss potential challenges in implementing this contract across a wider range of diverse provider APIs.

claims.text: Multimodal generation agents need more than a flat list of image URLs.
claims.source: General knowledge of multimodal AI systems and their current limitations.

claims.text: In GenFlick's movie and canvas workflows, the same uploaded image can mean identity, style, object, set, composition, mood, start frame, end frame, continuity source, edit source, motion reference, or audio reference.
Provider APIs differ sharply: some support labeled multi-image editing, some accept positional arrays, some use a single start frame, and some ignore references entirely.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Phased Planner-Executor Architecture for Multimodal Movie Generation Agents
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission focuses specifically on the structured handling of references within multimodal agents, a more granular problem than the overall architecture discussed in 2026-135.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Tail Reference Length and Dialogue Continuation in AI Video Extension
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission addresses a broader range of reference types and their application across various modalities (image, video, storyboarding), whereas 2026-142 is more specific to video extension and dialogue.
